title: Apples & Oranges
description: For each sale_date, find the difference between number of apples and oranges sold.
constraints: item is either 'apples' or 'oranges' — exactly one row per item per date
Every sale_date has exactly one apples row and one oranges row
key_concept: SUM(CASE WHEN ...) - SUM(CASE WHEN ...) or SUM with signed values.

hints.L1: For each date, you need counts of apples and oranges. Use conditional SUM.
hints.L2: SUM(CASE WHEN item = 'apples' THEN sold_num ELSE -sold_num END)
hints.L3: SELECT sale_date, SUM(CASE WHEN item='apples' THEN sold_num ELSE -sold_num END) AS diff FROM Sales GROUP BY sale_date ORDER BY sale_date

optimal_solution:
SELECT sale_date, SUM(CASE WHEN item = 'apples' THEN sold_num ELSE -sold_num END) AS diff
FROM Sales GROUP BY sale_date ORDER BY sale_date;
